title: The VALIS Trilogy — Page 380
raw_text:
Everybody. Big deal, I thought. With a drawing in the margin of someone
thumbing his nose. And for that, like this speech now, we paid good money.
You’d think having been poor so long would have taught me better, sharpened
my wits, as it were. My instinct for self-preservation.
I am the last living person who knew Bishop Timothy Archer of the Diocese
of California, his mistress, his son my husband the homeowner and wage
earner pro forma. Somebody should—well, it would be nice if no one went
the way they collectively went, volunteering to die, each of them, like
Parsifal, a perfect fool.
